context.projectOverview: agent-bober priority-hub spec — FINAL sprint. Register a hub team (data) so `bober chat hub` routes to the hub memory namespace, and add ADDITIVE /priority and /decide slash commands to the chat session that delegate to the Sprint 2-4 hub functions (collectFindings -> rankFindings -> renderPriorityMd) and return a ranked summary while writing priority.md. ALL pre-existing slash commands must remain byte-identical in behaviour.

completedSprints.title: hub module
completedSprints.summary: src/hub/: finding.ts, finding-source.ts (HUB_SCOPE), collector.ts (collectFindings), repo-resolver.ts (resolveSiblingRepos), scope.ts (parseScope), judge.ts (rankFindings(findings,scope,llm,now)), priority-md.ts (renderPriorityMd), hub-config.ts (resolveOutVault/priorityMdPath). src/cli/commands/hub.ts has runHubList + runHubPriority DI cores + list/priority/decide subcommands. DELEGATE to these — do NOT add new collector/judge/renderer logic.

relevantFiles: src/teams/registry.ts (team-as-data registry; add a hub team with memoryNamespace 'hub' + default pipelineShape; mirror the example team)
src/chat/slash-commands.ts (the dispatch — extend ADDITIVELY with /priority + /decide; keep all existing branches byte-identical)
src/chat/chat-session.ts:200-230 (slash-command dispatch from ChatSession; add handleHubPriority/handleHubDecide that delegate to hub functions)
src/cli/commands/hub.ts (runHubPriority DI core + collectFindings/resolveSiblingRepos/rankFindings/renderPriorityMd to delegate to)

principlesRef: .bober/principles.md (ESM .js extensions, import type, provider-agnostic createClient, teams-are-data, async fs, vitest collocated, conventional commits). CRITICAL: existing slash-command dispatch branches MUST stay byte-identical; new commands additive only; gate /priority and /decide so they are meaningful only in the hub namespace (informative no-op otherwise) — do NOT change default chat team behaviour. Make the new handlers testable with an injected/fake LLMClient + temp sibling stores (offline).
